channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: International peacekeepers killed as fighting rages around eastern Congo's key city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Fighting with M23 rebels in eastern Congo has left at least 13 peacekeepers and foreign soldiers dead, United Nations and army officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Belarus strongman set to win a 7th term in an election the opposition calls a farce
item.description: The smiling face of President Alexander Lukashenko gazed out from campaign posters across Belarus on Sunday as the country held an orchestrated election virtually guaranteed to give the 70-year-old autocrat yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more Palestinian refugees and floats plan to 'clean out' Gaza
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said Saturday he would like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting from the Gaza Strip, potentially moving out enough of the population to “just clean out” the war-torn area to crea
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Hermes menswear has a dash of jockey-style boldness
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Hermes illuminated a gloomy Paris afternoon with its fall men’s collection Saturday, as Veronique Nichanian turned to the racetrack for inspiration. Jockeys’ vibrant racing silks became her muse, a dynamic counterpoint to the French maison’s storied equestrian roots.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At Sundance, the hottest ticket in town was a Rose Byrne and Conan O’Brien psychological thriller
item.description: Rose Byrne plays a mother in the midst of a breakdown in the experiential psychological thriller “If I Had Legs I’d Kick You.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: More DEI fallout: Air Force scraps course that used videos of Tuskegee Airmen and female WWII pilots
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Air Force has removed training courses with videos of its storied Tuskegee Airmen and the Women Airforce Service Pilots, or WASPs — the female World War II pilots who were vital in ferrying warplanes for the military — to comply with the Trump administration's crackdown on dive
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, ABC 4

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, WGN 9

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kelli Ferrone wiped blinds and window sills inside her daughters’ elementary school, just a half mile from the devastating Palisades Fire that scorched the coastal enclave in Los Angeles. The cotton swabs and wipes came up black. There is scattered ash and soot at her feet.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship would overturn more than a century of precedent
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has said since his first administration that he wants to end birthright citizenship, a constitutional right for everyone born in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer And Janie Har, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump visit is 'morale boost': CA fire official
item.description: "I don't know what Trump can give us, but it's a morale boost," said Cal Fire battalion chief Brent Pascua.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Long Island’s last duck farm weighs closure after outbreak leads to killing of entire flock
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York’s Long Island was once synonymous with “duck” in the culinary world. Now it may lose its last commercial farm. The avian flu outbreak that has led to the slaughter of millions of birds at U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Trump cites voter ID, water flow as conditions for LA wildfire aid
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he wanted to see two actions taken in California before he offered federal support for Los Angeles as it grapples with wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 80 Guatemalan migrants deported from Biggs Army Airfield
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — The U.S. Department of Defense, along with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), deported 80 Guatemalan migrants on Friday morning, Jan. 24, from Biggs Army Airfield at Fort Bliss, U.S. Rep. Tony Gonzales, R-Texas said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests. It didn't fare well.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flame thrower and 'flusher' truck: Southern cities get creative to clear snow, ice
item.description: Days after a winter storm dropped ice and record-breaking snow, cleanup efforts were underway Thursday in several major Southern cities such as New Orleans, where crews were removing snow the same way they remove trash, drink cups and plastic beads after Mardi Gras.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE agents raid NJ seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Illegal immigrant admitted to killing woman on her 21st birthday
item.description: Ecuadorian native Jhon Moises Chacaguasay-Ilbis pleaded guilty to murdering 21-year-old Jocelyn Jhoana Toaquiza at a Syracuse, New York, Airbnb on her 21st birthday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: White House says migrant deportation flights with military aircraft have begun
item.description: The Trump administration has started flying immigrants who entered the U.S. illegally out of the country using military aircraft, a White House spokesperson said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Ford recalls over 270,000 Broncos and Mavericks: Here's why
item.description: Ford Motor Co. is recalling 272,827 Broncos and Mavericks due to a power problem that can cause vehicles to stop unexpectedly or fail to start.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: LeBron James picked for 21st straight All-Star Game, extending NBA record
item.description: LeBron James is extending his All-Star records. And Giannis Antetokounmpo is the people’s choice, again. The NBA revealed the starters — some of them, anyway — for the revamped All-Star Game on Thursday night, and there wasn't much in the way of surprises.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, ABC 4

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, ABC 4

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: The officer "had difficulty removing" the gun, and while trying to do so, it fired, striking the driver in the leg, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: ICE arrests nearly 500 in nationwide immigration crackdown that includes Illinois
item.description: Federal immigration agents conducted the first large-scale arrest operation under the Trump administration, resulting in nearly 500 arrests.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, WGN 9

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a new contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security Tuesday, firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up and eliminated all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4
